scores.correctness_notes: Covers the Python client (ops) side well — change model, statuses, wait_change, get_change, ChangeError, convenience methods. However, entirely omits CLI commands (pebble changes/tasks), API filtering (?select=in-progress), and the notices/long-poll mechanism, which the gold standard treats as key completion-checking methods.
scores.hallucination_notes: Minor issues: 'container.pebble' is not a documented public attribute on ops Container — the code example is flagged as uncertain even by the agent itself ('not quite'). 'client.replan_services()' after acquiring a supposed raw client mixes abstraction levels inconsistently. ChangeStatus.DOING exists but the attribution of all three statuses (DOING/DONE/ERROR) without verification is slightly uncertain. Not outright fabrications but minor inaccuracies.
